focus: Define a dependency-ordered acceptance matrix and failure-proof implementation boundary for the proposed standalone typed router.
noveltyJustification: 2 of 5 findings were fully new and 3 partially new; a 0.10 simplicity bonus for the dependency and atomicity model yields 0.80.
keyQuestions: What can break when moving from selectors to enumerated paths?
Which tests and benchmark gates prove behavioral parity and typed attribution?
Which changes must be atomic versus safely staged?
answeredQuestions: What can break when moving from selectors to enumerated paths?
Which tests and benchmark gates prove behavioral parity and typed attribution?

ruledOut: Treating support defaults as selected typed leaves or copying them into typed gold
Inferring the typed contract solely from heterogeneous compatibility resources
Landing typed gold or full-inventory behavior before producer-consumer seams are green

finding: Separate compatibility resources, selected typed leaves, support defaults, and navigation indexes.
A0-A9 cover baseline through promotion with exact pass conditions.
Atomic seams are contract-generation-readers, router-replay channels, and full-inventory-index semantics.
Map drift, specificity ties, ordering, broad-intent confusion, and default contamination each have a gate.
Promotion remains blocked until real pre/post reports satisfy A0-A9.
